kept.text:
GOOD NEWS 🚨 Elon Musk has officially settled his long-running legal battle with the SEC over his acquisition of Twitter (now X). To settle the score, a trust in Musk’s name will pay a $1.5 million fine 🔥

The case boiled down to an 11-day delay in Musk disclosing his 5% stake in the company. The SEC alleged this silence allowed him to keep buying shares at a lower price, saving him an estimated $150 million before the market could react to his involvement.

Under the deal, Musk admits no wrongdoing and gets to keep the $150 million the SEC says he saved. His legal team is framing the outcome as a "total clearance", allowing Musk to finally put these specific regulatory hurdles in the rearview mirror.

However, he isn't out of the woods yet. Musk still faces a massive $2.5 billion class-action lawsuit in San Francisco, where a jury recently found him liable for defrauding shareholders with his comments about "bots" during the buyout.

For the Tesla community, this is a major "clear the air" moment that ends years of friction with federal regulators. It signals an end to the 'Cold War' with federal regulators, clearing the path for Tesla’s next phase of growth 🚀

Source: Reuters

⚡⚡⚡最新消息：FSD 在荷蘭試駕主動避開駕駛沒看見的單車騎士

👉 德國 Tesla 車主 @teslanerdX 分享他在荷蘭 🇳🇱 的 FSD 試駕經驗：當車輛接近圓環時，FSD 突然自主煞車，原因是偵測到一位駕駛本人事先沒看見的單車騎士。

他表示：「這種時刻才真正展現這項技術的潛力。這不只是便利，關鍵時刻是能救命的。」

目前 Tesla FSD（Supervised）僅在荷蘭合法使用，4/10 經 RDW 核准後開始 OTA 推送。

✍️  FSD v14.3 的更新重點：強化對弱勢用路人（Vulnerable Road Users）的辨識能力，包括行人、單車騎士與小動物。

荷蘭是全球單車密度最高的國家之一，阿姆斯特丹的單車數量甚至超過汽車，FSD 能在這種環境通過 RDW 認證並順利運作，本身就是對視覺系統的嚴格考驗。

Tesla 的 360 度鏡頭感知不會疲勞、不會分心，能補上人類駕駛最大的盲點：注意力有限。當然這仍是 L2 駕駛輔助系統，駕駛人需保持監控與隨時接管的責任，但類似這種「車比人先看到」的真實案例，正是 FSD 在歐洲市場最有說服力的成績單。

kept.text:
Large battery takes longer to charge. That's why all of these new premium European EVs have 800V / 400kW systems. It's just 20 minutes from 10%->80%. I agree that you don't need 800km / 500 mile range. 400 miles / 650km is enough (assuming good aero, so that highway speed doesn't drop range as much). Tesla should bring their 800V/400kW system from Cybertruck to 3/Y to match the charging speed of new premium European cars.

Regardless what you think about the BMW iX3 or similar cars like Volvo EX60 and Mercedes GLC EQ, these are selling like hot cakes right now. EX60 has already 12 month queue here, and it's not even available for test drive yet. Volvo fans finally can buy a good EV with 800km WLTP / +340km/10min charging. Previous gen electric Volvos were not great. It's insane how bad previous gen Mercedes EQ-series was. The new generation must be the biggest generational jump ever: Mercedes has 2-gear gearbox, front engine disconnect and air suspension to lower the car to improve highway range. 2x more efficient heat pump, new much improved engines. CLA today beats Tesla 3 LR in efficiency. Mercedes used to be among the worst efficiency last gen. 

Volvo and BMW have gigacasting now to make the battery denser and cut weight, which made it possible for them to ship 800km WLTP cars with bigger batteries. You have to remember that these are premium cars, and premium customers want more. It doesn't have to be the most cost-efficient solution. Now people who want a premium car, can actually buy one that has more range and faster charging. Previous gen EU premium EVs were just flat out worse than Tesla in both aspects. Not anymore. Which is why these cars have 12+ month queues now in the EU. People with money have been waiting for EVs like this to appear in the market.

quoted_tweet.text:
The BMW iX3 and iX are examples of how little the company still understands about BEVs.

A very large battery means that in 99.99% of cases, when long distances aren't being driven, an unnecessary amount of weight is being carried around, which is inefficient and expensive.

Since a large battery naturally takes longer to charge, even with fast charging speeds, the supposedly advantageous feature of a long range is worthless, because you simply end up spending more time charging and if you take shorter charging stops you could have been more efficient with a smaller battery.

To everyone claiming they want to drive 1,000 km without a break: studies show that people with such a strong bladder are rare. And if your family is coming along, forget it. Such cases are so rare that it makes no sense to develop a vehicle for them.

Both vehicles are fundamentally flawed in their design and are only suitable for people who, as ICE owners, have been falsely instilled with fear for years that BEVs supposedly don't have enough range or that there aren't enough charging stations—both of which are lies perpetuated by the oil and gas industry.

kept.text:
I have some questions about Tesla's Robotaxi service rollout in Austin...

At first glance, https://t.co/pdMsLrJlKQ seems to be great for statistics on the rollout, but some key info is missing, mainly due to the way they source their data:

- How many cars are active in Austin at any one time? Cumulatively, 102 vehicles were spotted there (at the time of this post) since the launch of the service, but half of them haven't been spotted in the past month (the one-month interval since last-spotted is used by the site as the cutoff for deprecating a vehicle) and only 14 have been spotted in the last week

- How many unsupervised Robotaxis are active at any one time? Same story: a bunch of new license plates are spotted over a couple of days, but unsupervised vehicles from the "older" fleet are no longer seen.

- Is Tesla pulling the safety monitors from some of their supervised cars, making them "unsupervised", instead of adding new unsupervised vehicles to the fleet? It kinda makes operational sense for them to do that, but it also means the overall Robotaxi fleet is not growing much.

- Why does the service availability in Austin remain so poor, despite all these new vehicles being spotted? One potential explanation (although not confirmed) is that new vehicles are added while older ones are removed and no longer spotted by riders, which makes it look like the fleet is growing much more than it actually is.

The uberbulls are eager to celebrate each new spotted license plate, and particularly the Unsupervised ones, while completely ignoring the number of deprecated vehicles (the tracker is simultaneously good enough to spot new cars and too poor to spot most cars in the existing fleet, according to that logic) and the lack of improvement in service availability; they focus exclusively on the cumulative number rather than the more relevant Currently Active number (which isn't actually known).

Not trying to be a Debbie Downer, just looking for relevant, actionable info.

The TCMV meeting (117th session) in Brussels on May 5, 2026 concluded without a vote, as EU officials held an information session on Tesla’s “Full Self-Driving (Supervised)” system under Article 39.

According to Reuters, the Dutch vehicle authority RDW presented its preliminary approval, based on more than 1.6 million kilometers of testing on public roads and test tracks. The system has been authorized in the Netherlands since April 10, with the driver required to remain in control at all times.

Regulators raised several concerns during discussions, including:
• Vehicles exceeding speed limits
• Performance in icy conditions, particularly in Nordic countries
• Detection of large animals such as moose
• The use of the name “Full Self-Driving”
• Pressure from Tesla supporters contacting officials

Reuters also reported that some EU officials expressed more positive views based on early observations.

No vote took place during the session.

Source: Reuters

kept.text:
Teslas system får svensk kritik på flera viktiga punkter

Tesla vill få sitt ”självkörningssystem” godkänt – men europeiska myndigheter har hittat flera viktiga punkter som fått kritik, skriver Vi Bilägare:
https://t.co/2mqEhA6TYI

--
"Men flera länder har riktat kritik mot hur systemet fungerar, bland annat Sverige, rapporterar Reuters som begärt ut allmänna handlingar från europeiska myndigheter.

Kritiken handlar till exempel om hur systemet tillåter att bilen kör för fort, att det inte sänker hastigheten på hala vägar, att det inte hindrar föraren från att använda mobilen under körning och att namnet kan vara vilseledande.

Svenska Transportstyrelsen har sett ett ”begränsat” material om systemet, och där framgår att föraren själv kan ställa in en avvikelse som innebär att bilen kan köra för fort.

– Detta undantag strider i vår mening mot gällande regelverk för förarstödssystem, säger Anders Eriksson, som är teknisk utredare för automatiserade fordon på Transportstyrelsen, till Vi Bilägare.

Finns det andra tekniska brister ni vill se åtgärdade?

– Inget vi sett i dagsläget, men vi har inte fått ta del av godkännandeunderlaget utan bara fått övergripande presentationer från Tesla och RDW."

Min kommentar:
Vad mejlen som citeras i Reutersartikeln visar är inte på "brister i Teslas system", utan brister i tjänstemännens förståelse hur det fungerar. Funderingar om vinterväglag och hur det reagerar på en älg (!), till exempel. Och vad gäller mobilanvändning så är det en lös fundering, som är _helt felaktig_ utifrån Teslas kameraövervakning av föraren i FSD (Supervised) i Nederländerna.

Och vadå, förarstödssystem får inte köra för fort? Alla tillverkares farthållare går att ställa högre än tillåten hastighet, tex. Och lustigt nog, när Teslas FSD utvärderades inför testerna i Nacka så fick systemet mild kritik för att inte ha ökat hastigheten över hastighetsgränsen vid en omkörning!

kept.text:
The RDW presentation at the TCMV on May 5, 2026 was a crucial moment for Tesla FSD (Supervised) in Europe.

But this is where things get more complex.

The key question is how the RDW, the European Commission, and major member states like France and Germany interact in this process.

Here is how it works under Article 39 of Regulation (EU) 2018/858:

Do other countries still have a say?

Yes, absolutely.

Even though the RDW is the leading authority that technically approved the system, that approval is not enough for Europe-wide use.

• The approval is initially only valid in the Netherlands
• For EU-wide rollout, the European Commission must take an implementing decision
• This decision is made through the TCMV (Technical Committee – Motor Vehicles)
• All member states vote
• A qualified majority is required

This means countries like France and Germany have real influence.

If they push back:

• They can form a blocking minority or apply strong political pressure
• The European Commission can delay the decision
• Additional data or stricter requirements can be requested
• For example: more specific driver monitoring requirements

There is also another path.

Until there is an EU-wide decision:

• Individual member states can choose to recognize the Dutch approval
• Or they can refuse it

So in practice:

• FSD could be active in the Netherlands
• While remaining unavailable in countries that do not accept it

Why do France and Germany matter so much?

• They are two of the largest automotive players in Europe
• They have strong domestic industries (Volkswagen, Mercedes, Renault)
• They are generally more conservative in regulation

Germany:

• Strong focus on the strict boundary between driver assistance and autonomy

France:

• Often critical of how US tech companies apply European rules

So this is not just a technical process.

It is political
It is industrial
It is strategic

What do you think will happen next?

Will Europe align quickly
Or will countries like France and Germany slow this down? $TSLA

🚨Starlink, 미국 전역에서 ‘틈새 위성 인터넷’에서 ‘실질적 광대역 경쟁자’로 진화 중

1️⃣ Starlink의 미국 내 다운로드 속도가 크게 개선됐습니다.
Ookla의 최신 보고서에 따르면, 알래스카를 제외한 미국 거의 모든 주에서 Starlink Speedtest 사용자의 중간 다운로드 속도가 100Mbps를 넘었습니다.

2️⃣ FCC 광대역 기준 충족 비율도 크게 상승했습니다.
다운로드 100Mbps·업로드 20Mbps라는 FCC 최소 광대역 기준을 충족한 Starlink 사용자는 44.7%로 증가했습니다. 이전 17.4%와 비교하면 상당한 개선입니다.

3️⃣ 업로드 속도 개선도 중요한 변화입니다.
2024년에는 20Mbps 업로드 기준을 충족한 주가 없었지만, 이번 보고서에서는 22개 주가 해당 기준에 도달했습니다.

4️⃣ 기존 GEO 위성 사업자와의 격차가 커지고 있습니다.
Starlink의 중간 다운로드 속도는 Hughesnet보다 60% 이상, Viasat보다 65% 이상 높았습니다. 업로드 속도는 격차가 더 커서 Hughesnet보다 80% 이상, Viasat보다 95% 높았습니다.

5️⃣ 지연 시간도 개선되고 있습니다.
Starlink는 전국적으로 지연 시간을 낮추고 있으며, 40ms 미만 지연 시간을 기록한 주가 기존 1개에서 10개 주로 늘어났습니다.

6️⃣ 핵심은 Starlink의 포지션 변화입니다.
이제 Starlink는 단순히 인터넷이 닿지 않는 지역을 위한 보조 연결망이 아니라, 일부 지역에서는 기존 광대역 인터넷과 직접 경쟁할 수 있는 수준으로 올라서고 있습니다.

SpaceX's Starlink has made huge performance gains recently, according to a new report from Ookla:

 "@Starlink has dramatically improved its download speed performance across all 50 states. In fact, Speedtest users on Starlink are now able to get median download speeds exceeding 100 Mbps in every state but Alaska. 44.7% of Speedtest Starlink users were able to receive the FCC’s minimum threshold for broadband of 100/20 Mbps, making the provider look a lot less like a niche connectivity provider for unserved areas & more like a legitimate broadband competitor."

GEO satellite providers Hughesnet and Viasat are increasingly losing ground to Starlink in speeds and latency. Starlink’s median download speeds are now more than 60% higher than Hughesnet’s and more than 65% higher than Viasat’s. Median upload speeds show an even greater disparity with Starlink’s upload speeds more than 80% higher than Hughesnet’s upload speeds and 95% higher than Viasat’s.

Highlights from the report:
• 100+ Mbps download now in nearly every state (vs. 23 states in 2024)
• 20 Mbps upload reached in 22 states (vs. 0 before; FCC minimum)
• 44.7% meet full broadband standard (up from 17.4%)
• Latency improved nationwide; 10 states now under 40 ms (vs. 1)

🚨노르웨이 EV 점유율 98.6%, 테슬라 모델 Y는 또다시 베스트셀러

1️⃣ 2026년 1분기 노르웨이 신차 시장에서 플러그인 EV 점유율은 98.6%를 기록했습니다.
전년 동기 95.2%에서 더 상승한 수치입니다. 이 중 순수 전기차 BEV는 97.9%, 플러그인 하이브리드 PHEV는 0.7%였습니다. 

2️⃣ 노르웨이는 사실상 내연기관차 시장이 거의 사라진 단계에 들어섰습니다.
2026년 1분기 휘발유차와 일반 하이브리드를 합친 판매량은 78대, 점유율은 0.29%에 불과했습니다. 디젤도 월평균 100대 미만 수준으로 줄었습니다. 

3️⃣ 전체 신차 판매량은 27,175대로 전년 대비 약 14% 감소했습니다.
이는 2026년 1월부터 모든 차량에 더 높은 VAT가 적용되고, 일부 BEV의 VAT 면제도 종료되면서 2025년 말 선구매 수요가 발생한 영향으로 해석됩니다. 

4️⃣ 테슬라 모델 Y는 2026년 1분기 노르웨이에서 가장 많이 팔린 전기차이자 전체 자동차 베스트셀러였습니다.
CleanTechnica는 모델 Y가 본격 판매에 들어간 2021년 3분기 이후 매 분기 노르웨이 판매 1위를 유지하고 있다고 설명했습니다. 

5️⃣ 테슬라 모델 3는 2위를 차지했고, 토요타 bZ4X가 3위에 올랐습니다.
토요타 bZ4X는 2025년 하반기 개선된 주행거리와 DC 충전 성능, 그리고 노르웨이 시장에서 강하게 작용하는 10년·100만 km 배터리/파워트레인 보증이 판매에 영향을 준 것으로 분석됐습니다.

6️⃣ 기사에서는 토요타가 노르웨이 BEV 시장에 더 적극적으로 진입하고 있다고 평가했습니다.
bZ4X뿐 아니라 신형 C-HR BEV, Urban Cruiser 등이 등장하면서 토요타의 전기차 라인업이 확대되고 있습니다. 

7️⃣ 핵심은 노르웨이가 더 이상 ‘전기차 전환 중’인 시장이 아니라는 점입니다.
BEV 점유율이 98% 수준에 접근하면서, 노르웨이 신차 시장에서는 전기차가 사실상 기본 선택지가 되었고 내연기관차는 매우 작은 틈새 시장으로 축소됐습니다.

quoted_tweet.text:
WattEV has just announced that it is deploying 370 @Tesla Semi trucks, the largest single electric truck deployment in California.

• ~$100 million worth of Tesla Semis
• First 50 Tesla Semis to be delivered in 2026, full fleet by end of 2027
• 300+ of the Tesla Semis will be deployed under a joint program with the Port of Oakland
• New Megawatt charging hubs coming in Oakland, Fresno, Stockton & Sacramento
• Built to scale zero-emission long-haul freight across key CA corridors

“We selected the Tesla Semi based on cost, performance and availability after issuing a public request for proposals,” Salim Youssefzadeh, WattEV’s CEO, announced at the annual ACT Expo industry trade show in Las Vegas today.

"With the Tesla Semi now entering mass production and drawing strong reviews from fleet operators nationwide, WattEV’s vertically integrated model – combining vehicle deployment, megawatt-class charging infrastructure, and full-service leasing – offers a turn-key path for carriers without any capital risk."

More than 300 of the Tesla Semis will be deployed under a joint program with the Port of Oakland. Delivery of the first 50 Semis coincides with WattEV’s planned opening of truck-charging stations at the Port of Oakland and in Fresno, both equipped with Tesla’s Megawatt Charging System chargers capable of providing 300 miles of range to a Semi in approximately 30 minutes, comparable to a conventional diesel fill-up. Additional depots are scheduled to open this year in Stockton along with Sacramento breaking ground in 2026.
